meta.the_one_change: co-train on MULTIPLE domains jointly, test on a HELD-OUT one (vs re-gate single-domain train)
meta.pass_rule: held-out cross speed R2>0.9 AND yaw R2>0.9 AND ADE@2s < 1.5x in-domain heldout ADE@2s

overall_verdict.gate: held-out cross speed R2>0.9 AND yaw R2>0.9 AND ADE<1.5x in-domain
overall_verdict.primary_heldout_rigB: FAIL — multi-domain light-FT speed R2 -1.61 vs single-domain -1.65 (NO recovery); frozen -3.43 vs -3.21
overall_verdict.symmetric_heldout_comma: FAIL — multi-domain light-FT speed R2 0.452 vs single-domain 0.411 (+0.04, marginal); far from 0.9
overall_verdict.data_diversity_hypothesis: REFUTED on existing assets — adding a 2nd training domain does not recover held-out-rig/corpus transfer
overall_verdict.committed_reading: FAIL => the cross-rig/domain collapse is REPRESENTATIONAL, not cheap data-diversity. Pre-registered justification for the expensive path: a video-SSL (V-JEPA2-style) encoder with explicit geometry, or genuinely many-rig data acquisition + a metric-scale head. Do NOT proceed to YouTube on frozen/light-FT/2-domain-cotrain.
overall_verdict.triangulation: consistent with V-JEPA2-AC (arXiv:2506.09985 limitations): 1M+ h SSL also does not buy rig-invariance for free — rig-robustness must be engineered, not assumed from diversity or scale.
